details.body:
finding_summary: The branch makes `## Nearby work` a required `bead_execution_prompt/1` section without a compatibility path for the older `/1` prompts that are already persisted on existing tasks. `BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_VERSION` is still `1`, but `SECTION_NEARBY_WORK` was added to `BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_SECTION_TITLES` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:18-19,42-53`, and the renderer now always emits that section in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1217-1231`. Older version-1 prompts generated before this bead therefore fail `validate_canonical_prompt_shape`, which disables canonical scope handling in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:519-521` and causes prompt-review / override validation to reject unchanged historical prompts in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:426-435`.
remediation_hint: Either introduce a new contract version and keep consumers able to recognize/validate legacy v1 prompts, or keep `Nearby work` optional when validating older `/1` prompts.

finding_summary: `build_nearby_bead_context()` is treating hierarchy links as nearby dependency work. It passes the full `bead.dependencies` and `bead.dependents` arrays into `nearby_entries_from_relations()` (`src/cli/project.rs:2434-2437`), and that helper never filters by `DependencyKind` (`src/cli/project.rs:2457-2480`). Any bead with a `parent_child` link to its epic will therefore show that epic under `## Nearby work` as a direct dependency, and epics can likewise surface child beads as direct dependents. That misstates the relationship the section is supposed to convey and spends the nearby-context budget on hierarchy metadata instead of actual dependency/sibling context.
affected_files: src/cli/project.rs:2434-2480
remediation_hint: only include `DependencyKind::Blocks` edges in the direct dependency/dependent subsections; keep `parent_child` links for parent/sibling inference only.

finding_summary: the new `## Nearby work` section is not actually preserved by canonical-prompt validation. `is_optional_canonical_section()` marks `Nearby work` optional for every `bead_execution_prompt/1` prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:55-58`), and `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` therefore accepts prompts that omit it (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:342-346` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:388-392`). Prompt-review drift detection relies on that validator (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339`), so a rewritten prompt can silently drop the new section and still be accepted as canonical. That makes the feature non-durable in normal workflows: the prompt starts with nearby context, then prompt review can remove it without any rejection.
affected_files: src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:55-58,342-346,388-392; src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339
remediation_hint: either bump the contract version for the new shape or keep legacy compatibility in read-only consumers while requiring `## Nearby work` on newly generated/refined canonical prompts.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:426-435` now require `validate_current_canonical_prompt_shape`, which rejects canonical v1 prompts that legitimately omit `## Nearby work`. That bypasses the compatibility path intentionally added in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:298-306`, so prompt review and `project create-from-bead --prompt-file ...` can no longer accept previously generated canonical task prompts unless the caller invents a Nearby work section it was never given. Keep the stricter validator for freshly generated prompts, but preserve legacy-compatible validation when the source prompt predates this section or version-bump the contract before making the new heading mandatory everywhere.

`src/cli/project.rs:2457-2460` drops every non-`blocks` relation from `Nearby work`, so parent/child edges never appear in `direct_dependencies` or `direct_dependents`. Because bead creation still accepts any open/in-progress bead type (`src/cli/project.rs:1396-1423`), epic beads lose all child-bead scope summaries in `Direct dependents`, and child beads lose their parent epic in `Direct dependencies`, even though the bead spec says these subsections should summarize the focal bead's direct dependency/dependent IDs. If you want to avoid duplicate parent-epic noise for leaf beads, that needs a narrower rule than excluding the entire `parent-child` relation class.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:427-430` explicitly accepts contract-marked prompt overrides that omit `## Nearby work`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340` also treats that legacy shape as valid. But `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:182-190` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:366-367` still inject guidance that says `Nearby work` is an authoritative canonical section whenever the marker is present. On a supported legacy override, prompt-review/completion/final-review agents are told to preserve and use a section that does not exist, which can create false contract-drift or scope complaints.

`src/cli/project.rs:2586-2606` never truncates `NearbyBeadEntry.title` (and even falls back to the title for `scope_summary`), while `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:92-111` can only get back under budget by dropping whole entries and `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:140-148` renders the full title verbatim. A single long bead title can therefore blow the nearby-work byte budget or cause the highest-priority direct dependency to be removed entirely instead of being shortened, violating the per-entry and section-budget requirements.

finding_summary: The new `Nearby work` IDs cannot actually be used for `planned-elsewhere` routing. Review-stage guidance is still built from `extract_pe_bead_ids` only (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:191-195`), the classifier prompt still says `planned-elsewhere` only applies to beads listed under `Already Planned Elsewhere` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:417-425`), and `final_review.rs` strips any `mapped_to_bead_id` that is not in that PE-only allowlist before voting and again before restart routing (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1111-1123`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1844-1858`). When a prompt has Nearby work but no PE entries, every mapping to a sibling/dependent/related bead is discarded, so this bead's core end-to-end behavior still does not work.

finding_summary: Sibling inference and one-line scope summaries currently depend on `br list` returning fields that the load path does not guarantee. `load_bead_summaries` only loads `br list --all --deferred --limit=0 --json` (`src/cli/project.rs:1837-1852`), but `nearby_sibling_entries` reads `summary.dependencies` to recover the parent epic (`src/cli/project.rs:2492-2522`, `src/cli/project.rs:2579-2585`) and nearby entry rendering reads `summary.description` for the scope line (`src/cli/project.rs:2473-2480`, `src/cli/project.rs:2611-2617`). Those fields are optional/defaulted on `BeadSummary` (`src/adapters/br_models.rs:253-267`), so a summary-shaped list response silently yields no siblings and title-only scope summaries, with no fallback.

finding_summary: `Nearby work` can still include closed/deferred siblings when the builder is relying on parent-detail fallback rather than `br list` summaries. In `load_nearby_dependency_details`, sibling prefetching only filters `parent_detail.dependents` by `relation.status` at [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:1897), and `nearby_sibling_entries` repeats the same summary/relation-only check at [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:2690). But `nearby_entry_from_relation` later prefers the fetched bead detail status at [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:2619). If the parent relation omits status and the sibling summary is missing/sparse, a closed sibling loaded via `br show` is still admitted, rendered as closed in `## Nearby work`, and can even consume one of the 5 sibling slots ahead of an open sibling. That violates the requirement to omit closed/deferred nearby beads entirely and can mislead planned-elsewhere routing.
remediation_hint: Filter sibling candidates using detail status when it is available, both before the prefetch `take(NEARBY_SIBLING_LIMIT)` and before inserting the rendered sibling entry, and add a regression test where only the fetched sibling detail carries `status=closed`.

finding_summary: `Nearby work` can still surface closed/deferred beads under `Related work` when `br list` is stale. In `src/cli/project.rs:2755-2766`, related-work selection only filters on `BeadSummary.status`, even though `src/cli/project.rs:1956-1964` prefetches `br show` details for those same candidates. If the summary says a related bead is open but the detail says `closed` or `deferred`, the prompt still renders it (now with the closed status) and downstream review/final_review routing will treat that bead as a valid planned-elsewhere target.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:1956-1964
- src/cli/project.rs:2755-2827
remediation_hint: Filter related-work candidates with `nearby_candidate_status_is_omitted(...)` using `bead_details` before applying the top-3 limit, and add a regression test for summary-open/detail-closed related beads.

finding_summary: Prompt-review drift validation does not preserve the legacy prompt shape. In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340`, when the original prompt is a legacy v1 prompt without `## Nearby work`, the refined prompt is checked with the compatibility validator, so a refiner can add a brand-new `## Nearby work` section and still pass validation. That section is supposed to be graph-derived builder output, not free-form prompt-review text; once accepted, any fabricated nearby bead IDs become valid planned-elsewhere routing targets in later review stages.
severity: low
affected_files:
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340
remediation_hint: When the original prompt is legacy-without-nearby, require the refined prompt to stay on that same section shape unless there is an explicit migration path that rebuilds nearby-work content from bead data.

src/cli/project.rs:1912-1928 and 2788-2800 cap summary-inferred sibling detail prefetch at five IDs before detail filtering. When the parent epic detail cannot be loaded and one of those first five summaries is stale-open but actually closed, that sibling is dropped without promoting the sixth open sibling, so the `Nearby work` section can under-report available siblings and hide legitimate planned-elsewhere ownership.

src/cli/project.rs:1863-1966 and 2773-2785 now eagerly and sequentially run `br show` for every dependency/dependent, the parent epic, up to 20 sibling candidates, and a bounded related-work set before rendering the prompt. On moderately connected beads this turns task creation into dozens of subprocesses, making prompt creation latency scale with graph degree and label density.

`src/cli/project.rs:2631-2647, 1942-1957, 2815-2840` — the new nearby-context detail prefetch does not choose candidates using the same de-duplication rules that rendering uses. `included_ids` removes direct dependencies/dependents before siblings/related work are rendered, but sibling/related detail prefetch still only grabs the first fixed `limit+1` raw candidates. If two or more early matches are already consumed by higher-priority sections, the first actually rendered sibling/related entry never gets a `br show` refresh, so `nearby_candidate_status_is_omitted` falls back to stale summary data and can leak a closed/deferred bead back into `Nearby work`. That violates the bead’s closed/deferred filtering requirement and can misroute planned-elsewhere review findings.

`src/cli/project.rs:2905-2938`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:556-560`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:439-441`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1119-1124` — nearby related work is gathered across all bead summaries without milestone scoping, but the new routing allowlist/public guidance also adds short-form aliases for every nearby bead. If `Nearby work` includes a foreign bead like `other-ms.bead-4`, reviewers are told bare `bead-4` is a valid `mapped_to_bead_id`, and final review accepts it. Downstream planned-elsewhere verification resolves bare aliases by prepending the active bead’s milestone prefix, so that mapping can be recorded/commented against `current-ms.bead-4` instead of the foreign nearby owner. That silently misroutes the finding and can let the active bead ship with the wrong work marked as already covered.

finding_summary: equivalent planned-elsewhere targets are merged as conflicts when reviewers use different allowed aliases for the same nearby bead. `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:556-567` adds both canonical and short-form nearby IDs to the allowlist, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:439-441` exposes every allowed form to reviewers, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:541-584` compares `mapped_to_bead_id` as raw strings during amendment merge. If one reviewer writes `9ni.6.5` and another writes `6.5`, the merge downgrades the amendment to `fix-now` even though both point at the same bead. affected_files: `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`. remediation_hint: canonicalize `mapped_to_bead_id` against the prompt allowlist before merging/voting, or stop advertising short aliases in reviewer guidance.

finding_summary: Nearby-work status filtering still trusts `br list` too early, so the prompt can surface the wrong beads. Direct dependencies/dependents only prefetch `br show` detail when the summary text is sparse (`src/cli/project.rs:3013-3054`), while sibling/related scans discard `closed`/`deferred` summaries before a detail refresh can prove they are actually open (`src/cli/project.rs:3064-3153`). The renderer then uses whatever status survived that pipeline (`src/cli/project.rs:2771-2849`). If `br list` and `br show` disagree, closed direct relations can leak into `## Nearby work` or open nearby beads can disappear, violating the bead's filtering contract and hiding/creating the wrong planned-elsewhere targets.
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:2771-3054
- src/cli/project.rs:3064-3153
remediation_hint: Decouple status verification from title/description sparsity. Refresh candidate detail for status-sensitive nearby entries within the existing scan limits, and do not omit summary-closed candidates until the detail pass has had a chance to confirm their real state.

finding_summary: Prompt review is allowed to rewrite the graph-derived `## Nearby work` body, and final review then trusts the rewritten IDs as authoritative routing targets. The prompt-review guidance only requires the contract marker and section headings to survive (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:284-301`), and `canonical_contract_drift_concerns` only revalidates shape (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-350`). Later, final review builds the planned-elsewhere allowlist directly from the prompt text (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2364-2369`). A refiner can therefore delete real nearby beads or hallucinate new ones without tripping validation, which can suppress a needed restart by misclassifying a finding as planned-elsewhere.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:284-301
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-350
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2364-2369
remediation_hint: Treat `## Nearby work` like `AGENTS / Repo Guidance`: preserve it verbatim during prompt review, or regenerate/reconcile it from the bead graph before accepting a refined prompt.

finding_summary: `project create-from-bead` silently degrades the new Nearby work feature when `br list` cannot be loaded. In `src/cli/project.rs:419-423` an `AppError::Io` from `load_bead_summaries` is converted into an empty `BTreeMap`, and the new nearby-context builder in `src/cli/project.rs:2747-3009` depends on that map for sibling and related-work discovery. A transient `br list` failure therefore still creates a canonical bead task prompt, but without the sibling/related context reviewers are supposed to use for planned-elsewhere routing, with no indication to the operator that the prompt is incomplete.
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs
remediation_hint: Treat summary-load I/O failures as create-from-bead failures for canonical bead prompts, or surface an explicit degraded-state path instead of silently emitting an incomplete Nearby work section.

finding_summary: Nearby-work detail refresh silently discards every `br show` failure and then renders from stale summary/relation data as if the refresh succeeded, which can reintroduce closed/deferred beads or outdated titles/descriptions into `Nearby work`. `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2156` converts each `load_bead_detail` error to `None`, while `src/cli/project.rs:2776-2837` and `src/cli/project.rs:3171-3203` fall back to summary/relation status whenever detail is missing. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2156`, `src/cli/project.rs:2776-2837`, `src/cli/project.rs:3171-3203`. remediation_hint: propagate nearby detail-load failures, or conservatively omit entries whose authoritative detail could not be loaded instead of silently trusting stale summary data.

finding_summary: Nearby-work hydration now fans out one unbounded `br show` call per direct dependency/dependent before any production cap is applied, so highly connected beads can spawn dozens or hundreds of subprocesses during project creation. `src/cli/project.rs:1875-1886` feeds all IDs from `nearby_all_direct_relation_detail_candidate_ids`, `src/cli/project.rs:3076-3090` has no runtime limit, and `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2150` launches them all concurrently via `FuturesUnordered`. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:1875-1886`, `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2150`, `src/cli/project.rs:3076-3090`. remediation_hint: enforce a real runtime cap and/or semaphore around per-bead detail fetches, and prefer summary data for noncritical candidates.

`src/cli/project.rs:3290-3323` hard-filters `related_work` candidates to the active milestone, so a bead in another milestone that shares the same labels/subsystem never appears in `## Nearby work`. That breaks the main purpose of this bead for cross-milestone ownership: the final-review router already accepts canonical nearby IDs from any milestone (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:560-584`), but the prompt builder prevents those owners from ever being surfaced. Reviewers will misclassify those findings as `fix-now` or `propose-new-bead` because the owning bead is missing from the only prompt sections they are allowed to map against. Affected files: `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`. Remediation: remove the milestone-scope filter from related-work selection and keep the same-milestone restriction only for generating short aliases.

`src/cli/project.rs:1893-1904` pre-marks direct relations beyond `NEARBY_DIRECT_DETAIL_PREFETCH_LIMIT` as unavailable, and `src/cli/project.rs:2899-2914` then drops any direct dependency/dependent whose detail was capped or whose `br show` failed. That means high-priority nearby owners can disappear from `## Nearby work` before the byte-budget logic ever runs, even though their IDs are already present on the focal bead itself. Because `planned-elsewhere` routing only whitelists IDs from `Already Planned Elsewhere` and `Nearby work`, those skipped direct relations become unroutable in review despite still being real adjacent work. Affected files: `src/cli/project.rs`. Remediation: keep rendering direct relations from bead/relation summary data when detail refresh fails or is capped, and use detail only to refine status/description when it is available.

`src/cli/project.rs:418-421` now makes `project create-from-bead` fail on any `load_bead_summaries` error, and `src/cli/project.rs:1848-1853` maps a `br list` failure straight into that fatal path. Compared to the base branch's best-effort behavior, a transient `br list` problem now blocks bead-backed task creation entirely, even when `br show` can still resolve the active bead and its direct relations. That turns supplemental nearby-context hydration into a hard bootstrap dependency and makes task startup much more brittle. Affected files: `src/cli/project.rs`. Remediation: keep corrupt-record failures fatal, but degrade ordinary `br list` IO/exit failures to an empty/partial nearby context so project creation still succeeds.

finding_summary: A transient `br show` failure silently deletes sibling/related nearby context instead of falling back to the already-loaded summary. Real prompt generation enables `require_detail_for_entries` (`src/cli/project.rs:1891-1894`), `collect_nearby_detail_result` records any error as `unavailable` (`src/cli/project.rs:2199-2215`), and `nearby_candidate_status_is_omitted_with_policy` then suppresses every sibling/related bead without a detail row (`src/cli/project.rs:3336-3344`). Timeouts, parse failures, or other non-not-found errors therefore shrink or empty `Nearby work` nondeterministically even when `br list` already supplied enough data to render the entry, which can misroute later planned-elsewhere decisions.
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:1891-1894
- src/cli/project.rs:2199-2215
- src/cli/project.rs:3336-3344
remediation_hint: Treat transient detail-refresh failures differently from definitive missing/closed results, and fall back to summary rendering when the refresh does not succeed.

finding_summary: Direct dependencies/dependents after the first 24 IDs are never refreshed with `br show`, so stale open relation or summary data can still render closed/deferred beads in `## Nearby work`. This violates the requirement that closed/deferred nearby work be filtered out.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:1897
- src/cli/project.rs:2898
- src/cli/project.rs:3315
remediation_hint: Do not cap freshness checks for rendered direct relations; either refresh every direct relation's status before rendering, or omit uncapped direct relations when their status cannot be proven fresh.

finding_summary: Ambiguous short aliases are still accepted for `planned-elsewhere` mappings. When two allowed canonical bead IDs share the same short alias, conflict detection drops the alias from `canonical_by_allowed_id`, but `canonicalize_allowed` falls back to returning the raw alias anyway, so reviewers can persist a non-unique `mapped_to_bead_id` like `alpha`.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:466
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:515
remediation_hint: Track conflicting aliases explicitly and reject them during canonicalization, or remove ambiguous aliases from the allowlist instead of falling back to the trimmed raw string.

`src/cli/project.rs:2892-2912` drops a direct dependency/dependent from `## Nearby work` whenever its fresh `br show` detail is missing, even if the focal bead already carries that relation's id/title/status and `br list` supplied a summary. In the degraded paths this branch explicitly tries to support (`br list` unavailable, transient `br show` failure), the prompt can therefore render `No nearby open work was found.` while the old dependency block still lists those beads. Because final-review planned-elsewhere routing only allowlists IDs extracted from `Nearby work` / `Already Planned Elsewhere` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-470`), those omitted direct relations can no longer be mapped as planned-elsewhere. Fall back to relation/summary data for direct nearby entries when detail refresh is unavailable, and use fresh detail only to override stale status/description when it exists.

finding_summary: Direct dependencies/dependents can disappear from `Nearby work` when the per-bead refresh fails transiently and `br list` still has a stale `closed`/`deferred` summary. In `src/cli/project.rs:2906-2913`, `nearby_entries_from_relations` drops any direct relation for which `nearby_direct_relation_status_is_omitted` returns true, and `src/cli/project.rs:3310-3320` computes that omission status as `detail -> summary -> relation.status`. That ordering lets a stale summary override the focal bead's own relation status, even though the comment above the call says a missing refresh must not erase direct context. The result is that highest-priority nearby context can vanish and the reviewer/final-review prompts lose a valid planned-elsewhere target. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs`. remediation_hint: For direct relations, trust refreshed detail when present, otherwise prefer the focal bead relation status before consulting summary status.

finding_summary: The sibling/related-work refresh budget can be spent on candidates that are already known `closed`/`deferred` in summary data, which leaves later stale-open items unrefreshed and therefore renderable as open. `src/cli/project.rs:3257-3266` and `src/cli/project.rs:3194-3201` build prefetch candidate lists with `nearby_candidate_status_is_omitted_for_prefetch`, but `src/cli/project.rs:3368-3373` only checks already-loaded detail and `unavailable_ids`; it ignores the candidate's summary/relation status. Because the stabilizing loops cap detail loads (`src/cli/project.rs:2037-2054` and `src/cli/project.rs:2066-2082`), a backlog with many summary-closed siblings or related beads can exhaust the scan cap before later stale-open candidates are verified, so `Nearby work` may still show closed beads as open. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs`. remediation_hint: Exclude summary/relation-closed candidates from the prefetch lists and reserve the bounded detail scans for nominally-open entries whose status actually needs verification.

finding_summary: Open siblings and related beads can be omitted from `## Nearby work` when `br list` is stale and reports them as `closed`/`deferred`. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:3067-3100`, `src/cli/project.rs:3201-3210`, `src/cli/project.rs:3390-3404`. The sibling/related candidate filters consult summary status before any bounded `br show` refresh, so a summary-stale closed bead is never refreshed and the later detail-over-summary logic becomes unreachable for those categories.

finding_summary: Prompt review can fabricate a brand-new `## Nearby work` section when the original prompt is contract-marked but malformed rather than valid legacy v1. affected_files: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:337-350`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:361-368`. Only well-formed legacy prompts are blocked from adding `Nearby work`, and the verbatim-body check only runs when the original prompt already had that section, so a drifted prompt that kept the contract marker but lost `Nearby work` can be 'repaired' with hallucinated nearby bead IDs.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-500` no longer preserves the nearby-work short-alias safety rule from `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:560-573`. The prompt extractor only allowlists a nearby short alias when it belongs to the active milestone, but the canonicalizer rebuilds alias targets from every canonical bead ID whose suffix happens to match an allowlisted short alias. In a prompt that contains both `current-ms.bead-4` and `other-ms.bead-4`, reviewers are told that `bead-4` is valid for the local bead, yet `PlannedElsewhereIdCanonicalizer` marks it ambiguous and downgrades accepted `planned-elsewhere` findings back to `fix-now`. Preserve the same-milestone check when constructing alias targets (or derive the displayed allowlist from the canonicalizer) so foreign nearby beads cannot poison a local short alias.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:382-425` parses `## Nearby work` with an exact `line == "## Nearby work"` match, but the contract validator accepts canonical headings after `trim_end()` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:168-176`. A valid contract-marked prompt override that uses `## Nearby work ` (trailing spaces) will pass validation, but the new prompt-review guard will fail to locate the section and stop comparing the nearby-work body. In that state a refiner can rewrite or drop nearby bead IDs without tripping the 'preserve Nearby work verbatim' protection. Normalize heading lines the same way as the validator before extracting section bodies.

Restrict planned-elsewhere/nearby bead-id extraction to top-level canonical sections outside fenced blocks. The new nearby routing helpers call `extract_bullet_bead_ids_from_section`, which enters a section whenever `line.trim() == "## Nearby work"` / `"## Already Planned Elsewhere"` and then parses any trimmed `- ...` bullets until the next column-1 `##` heading ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:490-523]). Generated prompts deliberately indent canonical-looking headings inside AGENTS guidance ([src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:780-808]), so an indented or fenced example can now inject arbitrary bead IDs into the Nearby-work allowlist. That lets review/final_review accept `planned-elsewhere` mappings for beads that were never graph-derived.

Use summary/relation status when prefetching parent-detail-only siblings, or keep scanning until you have five non-closed candidates. In the sparse-summary fallback, sibling prefetching drops status information on the floor: the prefetch filter ignores `summary`/`relation_status` and only excludes candidates that already have a closed detail ([src/cli/project.rs:1973-1998], [src/cli/project.rs:2035-2118], [src/cli/project.rs:3054-3106], [src/cli/project.rs:3355-3402]). Because render mode later requires a loaded detail for siblings that exist only via `parent_detail.dependents` ([src/cli/project.rs:3373-3377]), a milestone with many closed parent-child siblings can consume the 10-load scan budget on closed IDs and omit later open siblings entirely. That breaks the bead's requirement to surface up to five open siblings after filtering closed/deferred ones.

Contract-marked prompt overrides can now invent `## Nearby work` IDs that review and final-review treat as valid `mapped_to_bead_id` owners. `create_project_from_bead_context` only shape-validates overrides ([src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:427-432]); later stages derive the planned-elsewhere allowlist directly from prompt text ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:191-195], [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-530, 2382-2385], [src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:591-632]). A stale or hand-written override can therefore silently reroute findings to beads that were never graph-derived for this task.

Prompt-review guidance becomes self-contradictory on malformed contract-marked prompts that do not actually contain `## Nearby work`. When validation fails, `prompt_authoritative_section_titles()` falls back to the full current section list ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:197-210]), and `prompt_review_consumer_guidance_for_prompt()` then tells the refiner to preserve the graph-derived nearby-work body verbatim ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:284-300, 338-342]). The validator later rejects any refinement that adds `## Nearby work` when the original prompt lacked it ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-370]), so drift-recovery can churn on impossible instructions.
